title: neoporteria subgibbosa f. microsperma
intro: Neoporteria subgibbosa f. microsperma is a rare, slow-growing species of cactus native to the Andes mountains in South America. It is known for its unique, small white flowers and spines, which are highly valued for their medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The microsperma form can be prepared as a herbal infusion or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and boost the immune system.
